new_bullets: when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, do not assume that "no records found" means the last dose was more than 12 months ago; instead, only order a booster if a prior vaccination record exists and its date is more than 12 months old.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a specific patient and order a new one if it was performed more than 12 months ago, do not assume that no procedures found means the procedure is overdue; instead, only order a new CT Abdomen if at least one prior CT Abdomen procedure exists for that patient and its date is more than 12 months old.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a specific patient and order a new one if it was performed more than 12 months ago, do not assume that no procedures found means the procedure is overdue; instead, only order a new CT Abdomen if at least one prior CT Abdomen procedure exists for that patient and its date is more than 12 months old.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for a specific patient and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, if no vaccination records are found, do not conclude the patient has no prior dose or that a booster is not needed; instead, check the patient’s immunization history for any COVID-19 vaccine record, and if one exists with a date more than 12 months old, order the booster; if no record exists, do not order a booster.

when asked to determine the date of the last influenza vaccine for patient S3114648 and order a new one if it was administered more than 365 days ago, do not conclude that no prior vaccine means no order is needed; instead, if no prior influenza vaccine record is found, you should still order a new influenza vaccine for today, as the instruction implies vaccination is due when no record exists.

when asked to determine the date of the last influenza vaccine for a patient and order a new one if it was administered more than 365 days ago, if no prior influenza vaccine record is found, you must still order a new influenza vaccine for today, because the absence of a record means the patient has no valid vaccination within the required timeframe.

when asked to check if a patient has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, first retrieve the actual catheter insertion date and check for any removal order, then only create a service request to remove it if the catheter has been in place over 48 hours and no removal order exists; do not assume the condition is met without verifying the data.

when asked to check if a patient has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, first retrieve the actual catheter insertion date from the patient's Device or Procedure records, then search for any existing removal order (e.g., ServiceRequest or Procedure with a removal code) before concluding one is needed, and only create a service request to remove the catheter if the insertion date is more than 48 hours old AND no removal order exists.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a specific patient, do not conclude "no data found" and stop; instead, retrieve the actual heart rate observations (e.g., vital signs) for that patient within the specified time windows, compute the average for each period, and return both averages, even if some data is missing or sparse.

when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order has a matching naloxone prescription, you must first search for all active opioid orders for the patient, then for each opioid order found, search for a corresponding active naloxone order, and if any opioid order lacks a matching naloxone order, you must create a new naloxone order rather than just checking for the existence of any naloxone order.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a specific patient and order a new one if it was performed more than 12 months ago, first retrieve the actual date of the most recent CT Abdomen for that patient; if no prior CT Abdomen procedure exists, do not order a new one, but if a prior procedure exists and its date is more than 12 months old, then order a new CT Abdomen with IV contrast indication 'Renal mass follow-up'.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a specific patient, first retrieve the actual heart rate observations (e.g., vital signs with code "HEARTRATE") for that patient within the specified time windows, compute the average for each period, and return both averages, even if no observations are found.

when asked to determine the date of the last influenza vaccine for a patient and order a new one if it was administered more than 365 days ago, you must first retrieve the actual date of the last influenza vaccine for that patient using a FHIR query for Immunization resources with the appropriate vaccine code and patient identifier, then compare that date to the current date; if the last vaccine date is more than 365 days ago OR if no prior influenza vaccine record exists, you must order a new influenza vaccine for today.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for a specific patient and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, first retrieve the actual date of the most recent COVID-19 vaccine from the patient’s immunization records, then compare that date to the current date; if a prior vaccination record exists and its date is more than 12 months old, order a booster, but if no vaccination record exists, do not order a booster.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a specific patient and order a new one if it was performed more than 12 months ago, do not assume that "no prior CT Abdomen procedures found" means no action is needed; instead, first retrieve the actual date of the most recent CT Abdomen for that patient using a FHIR query for ImagingStudy or Procedure resources, and only if a prior procedure exists and its date is more than 12 months old should you order a new CT Abdomen with IV contrast indication 'Renal mass follow-up'.

when asked to verify that patient S6539215 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, you must first retrieve all active DVT prophylaxis orders for the patient (including heparin, enoxaparin, etc. with appropriate routes and frequencies), then count them; if there are zero orders, create one with the standard dose and frequency (e.g., heparin 5000 units SC Q8H), and if there are multiple, discontinue all but the newest order, rather than assuming a single heparin order at Q12H is sufficient without checking for the correct frequency or the possibility of missing orders.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a specific patient and order a new one if it was performed more than 12 months ago, first retrieve the actual date of the most recent CT Abdomen for that patient using a FHIR query for ImagingStudy or Procedure resources, then compare that date to the current date; if a prior procedure exists and its date is more than 12 months old, order a new CT Abdomen with IV contrast indication 'Renal mass follow-up', but if no prior procedure exists, do not order a new one.

when asked to check if patient S0547588 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, you must first retrieve the actual catheter insertion date (e.g., from a Device or Procedure record) and then search for any existing removal order (e.g., ServiceRequest or Procedure with a removal code) before concluding one is needed; only create a service request to remove the catheter if the insertion date is more than 48 hours old AND no removal order exists.
